name: Pozo de los Humos Trail
name_original: Camino del Pozo de los Humos - Parque Natural de las Arribes del Duero
description: Jog the moderate 2.3-mile Pozo de los Humos Trail in Arribes del Duero Natural Park to see the spectacular 50-meter waterfall.
region_intro: Jogging around Pereña De La Ribera offers routes through a landscape characterized by river valleys and elevated viewpoints. The terrain often features significant elevation changes, leading to challenging running experiences. This region is known for its natural features, including waterfalls and scenic overlooks, providing varied backdrops for running. The routes primarily consist of difficult trails, catering to experienced runners. Best jogging routes around Pereña De La Ribera The most popular jogging route is Pozo de los Humos from Masueco Trail, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail that takes about 1 hour to complete. This route features a challenging ascent and leads to a prominent natural landmark. Another top favourite among local runners is Pozo de los Humos Trail, a moderate 2.3 miles (3.7 km) path. This shorter route offers a direct approach to the waterfall, with some elevation gain. Local runners also love the Rupurupay Viewpoint Trail, a 4.2 miles (6.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain to a scenic overlook, often completed in about 40 minutes. Jogging around Pereña De La Ribera is defined by river valleys, challenging ascents, and panoramic viewpoints, with options primarily for experienced runners seeking demanding trails. The routes in Pereña De La Ribera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 1.0 stars from 1 review. More than 30 runners have used komoot to explore Pereña De La Ribera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes around Pereña De La Ribera?
A: The running routes in Pereña De La Ribera are characterized by river valleys and elevated viewpoints. You can expect significant elevation changes, making for challenging running experiences. The terrain often includes natural features like waterfalls and scenic overlooks.

Q: Are there any easy running trails for beginners in Pereña De La Ribera?
A: Pereña De La Ribera's running routes are primarily designed for experienced runners, featuring difficult trails. There is one moderate route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993660762/pozo-de-los-humos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de los Humos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.7 km long and offers a direct approach to the waterfall with some elevation gain. However, there are no easy routes available.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for running routes in this area?
A: Running routes around Pereña De La Ribera vary in length. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993660762/pozo-de-los-humos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de los Humos Trail&lt;/a&gt; is about 3.7 km and takes around 25 minutes. Longer, more challenging routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993657961/pozo-de-los-humos-from-masueco-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de los Humos from Masueco Trail&lt;/a&gt; cover about 6.9 km and can take over an hour, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rupurapay Viewpoint – Aldeadávila de la Ribera loop from Aldeadávila de la Ribera&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 17.5 km, requiring several hours.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Pereña De La Ribera?
A: Yes, there are circular running routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rupurapay Viewpoint loop from Aldeadávila de la Ribera&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 8.8 km long and offers a challenging experience with significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the running trails?
A: The region is known for its panoramic viewpoints and scenic overlooks. Many routes lead to stunning vistas, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993663461/rupurupay-viewpoint-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rupurupay Viewpoint Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through varied terrain to a scenic overlook. You'll often encounter views of river valleys and dramatic landscapes.

Q: What natural landmarks or attractions can I see along the running routes?
A: Many running routes in Pereña De La Ribera offer access to impressive natural landmarks. You can visit the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4230399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de los Humos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy the vistas from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4791099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Staircase Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de los Humos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Another beautiful waterfall to discover is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4230380" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Pinero&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Pereña De La Ribera?
A: The running routes in Pereña De La Ribera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 1.0 stars. Over 30 runners have used komoot to explore the varied and challenging terrain, often praising the dramatic river valleys and rewarding viewpoints.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest near the running trails?
A: Yes, in addition to natural beauty, you can find cultural landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Cruz&lt;/a&gt; is a religious building that adds a historical touch to the area's attractions.

Q: How many running routes are available in Pereña De La Ribera?
A: There are 8 distinct running routes available around Pereña De La Ribera, offering a range of distances and difficulties, predominantly catering to experienced runners.

Q: When is the best time of year to go running in Pereña De La Ribera?
A: Given the region's significant elevation changes and natural features like waterfalls, spring and autumn are generally ideal for running in Pereña De La Ribera. The weather is typically milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summers can be hot, and winters might present colder conditions, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are the running trails well-marked?
A: While specific marking details vary by route, komoot users generally find the trails navigable. It's always recommended to download the route offline on your komoot app before heading out, especially on less-frequented or challenging paths.
tips: The Pozo de los Humos is an impressive waterfall located in the province of Salamanca, in the autonomous community of Castilla y León, Spain. This waterfall is located on the Uces River and is known for its spectacular waterfall of about 50 meters high, especially impressive during the rainy season. 😊

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Pozo de los Humos Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied and engaging experience. You'll encounter a mix of natural paths, gravel, and unpaved sections. There are also shorter segments of paved road and asphalt. Expect a dirt track that can be wide in parts, with some sections described as 'mountain hiking paths' and even 'alpine' terrain, especially on routes descending towards the waterfall.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Pozo de los Humos Trail for jogging?
faqs.answer: This jogging route is rated as moderate. While some paths to Pozo de los Humos can be very easy, this specific trail involves varied terrain and can include significant descents. A circular running route from Masueco, for example, is considered a 'Hard run' requiring good fitness, sure-footedness, and sturdy shoes. Be prepared for a dynamic workout.

faqs.question: What are the main natural features and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The undeniable centerpiece is the spectacular 50-meter high Pozo de los Humos waterfall, often called 'Spain's most beautiful waterfall'. When the Uces River has high water flow, especially during the rainy season, the waterfall creates a distinctive 'vapour cloud' or 'mist that looks like smoke'. You'll also find the Pozo de los Humos Viewpoint, offering impressive perspectives of the cascade and the dramatic canyon of the Arribes del Duero Natural Park.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Pozo de los Humos Trail?
faqs.answer: To witness the Pozo de los Humos waterfall at its most impressive, plan your visit during the rainy season, typically from December to May, or after intense rainfall. This is when the Uces River has high water flow, creating the iconic 'smoke' effect. The natural environment is enchanting year-round, but the waterfall's spectacle is seasonal.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Camino del Pozo de los Humos, Camino de Valdazar, and Camino de los Cuernos, offering a diverse experience within the natural park.

faqs.question: Is the Pozo de los Humos Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Arribes del Duero Natural Park generally welcomes dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash at all times. This is a natural reserve with protected bird species and other wildlife, so responsible pet ownership is essential to protect the ecosystem.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the trail or the Arribes del Duero Natural Park?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Pozo de los Humos Trail or the Arribes del Duero Natural Park itself. However, as it's a protected natural area, visitors are expected to respect the environment, stay on marked paths, and not disturb wildlife.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Pozo de los Humos Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is often accessed from nearby villages like Masueco. Parking facilities are typically available in these villages, providing convenient access points for the various routes leading to the waterfall. Always check local signage for specific parking regulations.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for jogging on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, including 'mountain hiking paths' and potentially 'alpine' sections, sturdy shoes with good grip are highly recommended. Dress in layers suitable for the weather, and bring enough water, especially if you plan a longer run. Sun protection is also advisable.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see in the Arribes del Duero Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The dramatic canyon walls of the Arribes del Duero Natural Park are a sanctuary for protected bird species. Keep an eye out for magnificent raptors such as golden eagles, griffon vultures, and black storks soaring above the precipitous cliffs. The park's indigenous vegetation also supports a diverse range of smaller wildlife.

faqs.question: Is there an 'insider tip' for experiencing the Pozo de los Humos waterfall?
faqs.answer: To fully appreciate the 'smoke' effect that gives the waterfall its name, aim to visit during the wetter months (December to May) or immediately after heavy rainfall. The increased water flow dramatically enhances the mist, creating a truly spectacular sight that lives up to its reputation.
